name: Alipay International
tagline: Recommended for most tourists — easiest foreign card setup

steps.title: Download Alipay
steps.description: Download the Alipay app from the App Store. Make sure you download the international version — search for 'Alipay' (blue icon). Do this before you land in China as App Store access may be needed.
tips: Use your home country App Store — not the Chinese one
The app icon is blue. Avoid the Chinese-only version with a different layout
App size is about 200MB — download on WiFi

steps.title: Create Your Account
steps.description: Open Alipay and tap 'Sign Up'. Enter your foreign phone number with your country code. You will receive an SMS verification code. Enter the code to verify.
tips: Use a phone number you can receive SMS on — your home SIM works for signup
If you don't receive the SMS, tap 'Resend' after 60 seconds
Save your login credentials — you'll need them in China

steps.title: Add a Foreign Bank Card
steps.description: Go to 'Me' → 'Bank Cards' → 'Add Card'. Enter your Visa or Mastercard debit or credit card details. Alipay supports most international cards issued outside China.
tips: Visa and Mastercard are both supported
Use a card with no foreign transaction fees if possible
Some prepaid cards may not be accepted — use a regular bank card
You may need to verify with a one-time charge from your bank

steps.title: Enable 'Tour Pass'
steps.description: Alipay has a special 'Tour Pass' feature for international visitors that allows you to top up a CNY balance using your foreign card. Go to the home screen and search for 'Tour Pass'. Load at least ¥200 to start.
tips: Tour Pass gives you a CNY wallet — essential for seamless payments
Minimum top-up is usually ¥100, maximum varies by account level
The balance does not expire during your trip

showAndPointCard.chineseText: 我是外国游客，请帮我开通支付宝国际版
showAndPointCard.context: Show to Alipay staff or a bilingual helper — asking for help activating Alipay International

steps.title: Pay with QR Code
steps.description: To pay, open Alipay and tap the 'Pay' button (付款码). Show the QR code to the cashier for them to scan. Alternatively, tap 'Scan' to scan the merchant's QR code yourself.
tips: Most shops have a QR code displayed at the counter — just scan it
Taxis and small vendors usually prefer you showing your QR for them to scan
Make sure you have enough CNY balance in Tour Pass before paying

showAndPointCard.context: Show to cashier — means 'please scan my payment QR code'

name: WeChat Pay
tagline: Widely accepted — requires WeChat account setup

steps.title: Download WeChat
steps.description: Download WeChat from the App Store. WeChat is both a messaging app and a payment platform. The payment feature is built in — no separate app needed.
tips: Download before you arrive in China
WeChat is also China's most popular messaging app — useful for contacting hotels
App size is about 300MB

steps.title: Create a WeChat Account
steps.description: Open WeChat and tap 'Sign Up'. Register with your foreign phone number. You will need to verify via SMS. Important: WeChat may ask an existing user to confirm your account — ask a friend who has WeChat, or use the in-app verification option.
tips: The 'security verification by a friend' step can be bypassed in some regions — try a few times
If you have a Chinese-speaking contact on WeChat already, ask them to verify you
Use your real phone number — WeChat requires SMS verification

steps.title: Set Up WeChat Pay with Foreign Card
steps.description: Go to 'Me' → 'Services' → 'WeChat Pay' → 'Wallet'. Tap 'Add a Card'. Enter your Visa or Mastercard details. WeChat Pay supports foreign cards for international users.
tips: Only Visa and Mastercard are supported — Amex is not accepted
Your card issuer may flag the transaction — notify them before your trip
A small authorization charge may appear and then be refunded

steps.title: Pay with QR Code
steps.description: To pay, go to 'Me' → 'Services' → 'WeChat Pay' → 'Pay'. Show your payment QR code to the cashier. Or tap 'Scan' to scan the merchant's code. Some merchants have a single QR code that accepts both Alipay and WeChat Pay.
tips: Look for the green WeChat Pay logo at checkout
If a merchant only accepts Alipay, you may need to use Alipay instead
Keep WeChat Pay open and ready — payments are fast once set up

showAndPointCard.context: Show to cashier — means 'please scan my WeChat Pay QR code'
